audit_items.proof_trace: At line 71, `p = ber_strdup(urlstr)` can return NULL on allocation failure (OOM), but there is no null check before `p` is passed to `ldap_pvt_hex_unescape(p)` at line 80, which immediately dereferences its argument (`*s != '\0'` at url.c:1625), causing a null pointer dereference. The same NULL `p` would reach `fopen(p, "rb")` at line 82, constituting undefined behavior. The proof that the property holds fails at line 80 because the OOM path is unguarded.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker sends a malformed LDAP Modify/Add request containing a URL-valued LDIF attribute (e.g., `< file:/etc/passwd`) to drive slapd into an OOM state; when `ber_strdup` in `ldif_open_url` returns NULL, `ldap_pvt_hex_unescape(NULL)` immediately crashes the slapd process, causing a denial of service.
